executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware wave with 103 organizations compromised in a single day across all critical sectors. The Qilin ransomware group leads the assault with 20 victims including Canada Goose, Eastern Townships School Board, and NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary). High-profile targets include National Money Mart (database leaked by Everest group), Peterborough Public Health, MRC de Maskinongé district government, Municipality of North Perth, and Kids & Company childcare services. The Play, Clop, and SafePay groups are each claiming 10+ victims, indicating coordinated or automated mass exploitation. This represents a national-scale cyber emergency requiring immediate incident response across transportation, healthcare, education, financial services, and public sector entities.

items.name: Qilin (Agenda)
items.targets: 20 Canadian organizations including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, A Lococo Wholesale, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Bergmanis Preyra law firm, Pre-Con Builders, Questica, Eastern Townships School Board, MG CPA, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon), Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric, Capp Shupak
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1078 (Valid Accounts). Double-extortion ransomware with leak site publication.

items.name: Play
items.targets: 12 Canadian organizations including Select Tool, Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Paisley Products, Makivik, ALLMAX Nutrition, Pewarchuk CPA, Security ONE Alarm Systems, Katch Kan, Turkstra Trusses
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1021 (Remote Services). Known for targeting MSPs and supply chain.

items.name: Clop (TA505)
items.targets: 13 Canadian organizations including Strategic Objectives, Ideal Welders, Conwest, CMH Heli, Granite Pointe, SMT Lawyers, Cloud Data Works, Sensible Solutions, Whiskijack Resorts, Technikel, Modtech, Eastern Platinum, Centaur Products
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1005 (Data from Local System). Mass exploitation through zero-day vulnerabilities in file transfer applications.

items.name: SafePay
items.targets: 10 Canadian organizations including Briway Carriers, Matt and Steve, RIPO Quebec, Groupe Piché, Precision Aluminum, Kenalex, Silverline Group, Teccart College, MacTavish Co
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), T1027 (Obfuscated Files or Information)

items.name: TheGentlemen
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Groupe Caddac, ControlGMC, Labtician Ophthalmics, Real Tech, Colliers
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1588.002 (Obtain Capabilities: Tool), T1204 (User Execution)

items.name: DragonForce
items.targets: 6 Canadian organizations including Phoenix Labs, CCR Solutions, Esposito Bros Construction, Soteck, McKee-Pownall Equine Services, Emond Publishing
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol), T1083 (File and Directory Discovery)

items.name: IncRansom
items.targets: 8 Canadian organizations including CPK Interior, Excavation Tourigny, Glassers TV, Steelworks Inc, Bison Family Medical, Omega Tool Corp, Reidhurst Nagy, Bais Yaakov school
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1048 (Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol)

items.name: Lynx
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Peterborough Public Health, Laurysen Kitchens, Group Echo, KTL Group, Trucash financial services
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1562 (Impair Defenses), T1041 (Exfiltration Over C2 Channel). Public health targeting indicates potential healthcare focus.

items.name: Sinobi
items.targets: 3 Canadian organizations including Active Green + Ross automotive, Morison Insurance Brokers, Kids & Company childcare services
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1070 (Indicator Removal), T1560 (Archive Collected Data)

items.name: AiLock
items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations including Solutions Extreme Technology, Eeyou Communications Network (indigenous telecom)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1489 (Service Stop), T1112 (Modify Registry)

items.name: Everest
items.targets: National Money Mart Company - full database exfiltration and leak confirmed
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567.002 (Exfiltration to Cloud Storage), T1213 (Data from Information Repositories). Database leaked publicly.

items.name: Medusa
items.targets: MRC de Maskinongé (regional county municipality in Quebec)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1047 (Windows Management Instrumentation), T1003 (OS Credential Dumping). Targeting municipal government infrastructure.

items.name: Worldleaks
items.targets: Municipality of North Perth, Ontario
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1566 (Phishing), T1105 (Ingress Tool Transfer). Municipal government targeting.

items.name: Akira
items.targets: Inland Audio Visual business services
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1021.001 (Remote Desktop Protocol), T1059.001 (PowerShell)

items.name: Payload
items.targets: Q2 Artificial Lift Services manufacturing
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1218 (System Binary Proxy Execution)

items.name: Nightspire
items.targets: 2 organizations including Regal Building Materials and one unidentified law firm
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1083 (File and Directory Discovery), T1560.001 (Archive via Utility)

items.name: Bravox
items.targets: John O's Foods agriculture/food production
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1133 (External Remote Services), T1055 (Process Injection)

items.name: Nitrogen
items.targets: Whitfield Welding Inc manufacturing
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1543 (Create or Modify System Process), T1027.002 (Obfuscated Files: Software Packing)

items.name: Securotrop
items.targets: 2 organizations including Living Realty and Cadman Power Equipment
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1082 (System Information Discovery), T1012 (Query Registry)

items.name: Devman
items.targets: 4 organizations including Beausejour Co-op, Axion50plus, Ravand Technology, and 2 others with redacted names
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1087 (Account Discovery), T1049 (System Network Connections Discovery)

items.name: Anubis
items.targets: One unidentified Canadian law firm
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1114 (Email Collection), T1039 (Data from Network Shared Drive). Legal sector targeting.

items.name: MedusaLocker
items.targets: Dulay technology company
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1562.001 (Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools), T1489 (Service Stop)

items.name: CoinbaseCartel
items.targets: Canada Goose luxury consumer brand
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File), T1071.001 (Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a national ransomware crisis with 103 organizations breached on May 5, 2026, spanning all provinces and critical sectors. Public sector entities hit include Peterborough Public Health, MRC de Maskinongé district, Municipality of North Perth, Workers Health & Safety Centre, and Eastern Townships School Board. The coordinated timing and scale suggest either mass exploitation of a common vulnerability or automated attacks leveraging compromised credentials/VPN access. No CCCS advisories were identified in this feed, indicating potential delayed government response to the emerging crisis.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose luxury retailer breached by CoinbaseCartel ransomware group. Active Green + Ross automotive retail chain compromised by Sinobi group. Kids & Company childcare services (national chain) hit by Sinobi.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (major financial services provider) suffered full database leak by Everest ransomware group. Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi. Trucash financial services breached by Lynx group. Multiple accounting firms hit including Pewarchuk CPA, MG CPA, and Reidhurst Nagy.

report_2w.executive_summary: Two critical vulnerabilities demand immediate patching: CVE-2026-31431 (Copy Fail) enables root privilege escalation across Linux cloud environments and is actively exploited; CVE-2026-41940 allows authentication bypass in cPanel/WHM, with 44,000 servers reportedly already compromised and ransomware cases emerging. Cybersecurity vendor Trellix disclosed a breach of its own source code repositories. Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued official advisories on the Linux kernel flaw, the cPanel vulnerability, and social engineering attacks targeting enterprise cloud software. Two cybersecurity professionals received 4-year prison sentences for BlackCat ransomware attacks, and a massive Facebook phishing campaign using Google AppSheet compromised 30,000 accounts.

items.description: Copy Fail vulnerability in Linux kernel crypto subsystem enables local privilege escalation to root across major distributions and cloud environments
items.software_affected: Linux kernel (algif_aead crypto subsystem) - multiple distributions including cloud environments
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Critical authentication bypass vulnerability in cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM) allowing unauthorized access
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Vulnerability impacting Cisco ASA and FTD devices
Vulnerability impacting Cisco ASA and FTD devices
Vulnerability impacting Cisco ASA and FTD devices
Apache path traversal vulnerability observed in active scanning campaigns
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: Medium, Availability: Low

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: NVIDIA GeForce NOW (alleged), ZenBusiness (5.1M accounts exposed)
items.ttps: T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information) - large-scale data exfiltration and credential harvesting

items.name: Everest (ransomware group)
items.targets: TSYS, Epiq Global, Silfab Solar
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - ransomware operations with data exfiltration and extortion

items.name: BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware affiliates
items.targets: Multiple victims - two cybersecurity professionals sentenced for participation
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1566 (Phishing), T1078 (Valid Accounts) - ransomware-as-a-service operations

items.name: CORDIAL SPIDER and SNARKY SPIDER
items.targets: SharePoint, HubSpot, and Google Workspace users
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link), T1539 (Steal Web Session Cookie) - adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) phishing pages for session hijacking

items.name: cPanelSniper (CVE-2026-41940 exploitation)
items.targets: cPanel/WHM servers - 44,000 servers reportedly compromised, ransomware cases emerging
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - authentication bypass leading to full compromise and ransomware deployment

items.name: Facebook phishing campaign (AppSheet-based)
items.targets: Facebook users - 30,000 accounts compromised
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link), T1078 (Valid Accounts) - leveraging Google AppSheet, Netlify, and Telegram for large-scale phishing operations

items.name: Digit (Russian hacker)
items.targets: Ukraine and United States
items.ttps: T1498 (Network Denial of Service), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) - cyberattacks targeting critical infrastructure

items.name: Shai-Hulud malware (Mini variant)
items.targets: Software developers using SAP CAP and Intercom npm packages
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain), T1555 (Credentials from Password Stores) - malicious npm packages executing credential theft at install-time, targeting dev machines and CI/CD runners

items.name: 313 Team (pro-Iran DDoS crew)
items.targets: Canonical (Ubuntu domain) - DDoS extortion campaign
items.ttps: T1498 (Network Denial of Service), T1657 (Financial Theft) - DDoS attacks with ransom demands

items.name: mnt6 (threat actor)
items.targets: Silfab Solar
items.ttps: T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - project files allegedly exposed (unverified claim)

items.name: Path Traversal scanner (São Paulo origin)
items.targets: Global infrastructure scanning for CVE-2021-41773, CVE-2021-42013, CVE-2017-9841
items.ttps: T1595.002 (Active Scanning: Vulnerability Scanning), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) - automated exploitation attempts

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued four official alerts in the past two weeks covering critical vulnerabilities (Linux CVE-2026-31431, cPanel CVE-2026-41940, Cisco ASA/FTD flaws) and warned about social engineering attacks targeting enterprise SaaS environments. A Maryland pharmacist was indicted for unauthorized computer access at University of Maryland Medical Center, and a 15-year-old in France was linked to a mega-breach at that country's secure document agency.
